briefdescription.para: This function detects the PHY address by looking for successful MII status register contents (PHY register 1).

detaileddescription.para: It looks for a PHY that supports auto-negotiation and 10Mbps full-duplex and half-duplex. So, this code won't work for PHYs that don't support those features, but it's a bit more general purpose than matching a specific PHY manufacturer ID.

Note also that on some (older) Xilinx ML4xx boards, PHY address 0 does not properly respond to this query. But, since the default is 0 and assuming no other address responds, then it seems to work OK.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the instance of EmacLite driver.

simplesect.para: The address of the PHY device detected (returns 0 if not detected).
The bit mask (0x1808) of the MII status register (PHY Register 1) used in this function are: 0x1000: 10Mbps full duplex support. 0x0800: 10Mbps half duplex support. 0x0008: Auto-negotiation support.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the MAC loopback on the PHY.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the instance of EmacLite driver.
is the address of the Phy device.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the loop back is enabled.
XST_FAILURE if the loop back was not enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the MAC loopback on the PHY.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the instance of EmacLite driver.
is the address of the Phy device.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the loop back was disabled.
XST_FAILURE if the loop back was not disabled.

briefdescription.para: For PPC we use a usleep call, for Microblaze we use an assembly loop that is roughly the same regardless of optimization level, although caches and memory access time can make the delay vary.

detaileddescription.para: Just keep in mind that after resetting or updating the PHY modes, the PHY typically needs time to recover.

briefdescription.para: This file implements the utility functions for the EmacLite example code.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
2.00a ktn 04/13/09 First release 2.00a ktn 06/13/09 Changed the EmacLitePhyDetect function so that the function is not in an infinite loop in case of a faulty Phy device. 3.03a bss 09/01/12 Moved the declarations of RecvFrameLength, TransmitComplete, EmacLiteInstance TxFrame[XEL_MAX_FRAME_SIZE], RxFrame[XEL_MAX_FRAME_SIZE] from the xemaclite_example.h to this file for fixing C++ compilation errors 4.9 ml 11/15/23 Fix compilation errors reported with -std=c2x compiler flag
